pilot_scope: ND-only pilot based on reqret-001; other cases deferred until the first case is reviewed.

output_contract.article_coverage: The model must return exactly one article_summaries item for every input article.
output_contract.irrelevant_policy: Irrelevant and distractor articles must be labeled explicitly and must not be converted into substantive supporting theses.
output_contract.request_specificity: Summaries and theses must explain what the article contributes to the Avito New Developments request, not provide a generic article recap.
output_contract.full_text_usage: The model may use body_full_text and body_excerpt, but should cite only article_id evidence, not quote long passages.

article_summaries.request_specific_summary: The article is an analogue for using first-party buyer behavior to prioritize follow-up, but it does not directly validate developer CPA pricing.

theses.statement: Behavioral intent signals can help prioritize sales follow-up.
theses.evidence: The article describes activity signals being used to decide which lead needs immediate follow-up.

article_summaries.avito_implication: Useful as an analogue for Avito ND lead scoring, with caution that the monetization model is not direct developer CPA proof.
caveats: Adjacent residential workflow evidence, not a direct new-development portal pricing case.
